reflections.subject: Mathematics
reflections.learner_response: Learners were able to compare fractions using strips.
reflections.challenges: A few learners needed extra time
reflections.next_steps: Add a quick warm-up before the lesson

reflections.subject: Math
reflections.strategies_used: Fraction strips, think-alouds, oral response options
reflections.learner_response: Learners stayed engaged when visuals were introduced.
reflections.challenges: A few learners needed extra time to interpret the fraction cards.
reflections.next_steps: Pre-teach the vocabulary and add a short review warm-up.

reflections.subject: English
reflections.learner_response: Most learners participated during the guided discussion.
reflections.challenges: Instructions needed to be repeated more than once.
reflections.next_steps: Chunk the task directions into smaller steps.

reflections.subject: Science
reflections.learner_response: Learners copied the routine quickly after modeling.
reflections.challenges: One group needed reminder prompts during cleanup.
reflections.next_steps: Add a cleanup checklist for each group table.

observations.subject: Math 5
observations.notes: Support strategies were visible throughout the activity.

observations.subject: Math 8
observations.notes: The lesson used visuals and partner support effectively.

observations.subject: English 9
observations.notes: Several learners needed more structured turn-taking prompts.

pre: I can plan lessons that accommodate learners with diverse needs.
I can modify lesson objectives appropriately for learners with SEN.
I feel confident selecting inclusive support strategies.
I can contextualize lessons to meet the needs of my diverse learners.
I know how to document reflections after teaching an inclusive lesson.
